methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $8.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.5B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Wayve is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Amar Shah and Alex Kendall, currently valued at $8.6B post-money following $2.5B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies spanning Series C through Series E, with one relevant public comp (Palantir, ticker PLTR). The peer set ranges from $2.5B to $39.0B, with a median of $7.9B.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $12.9B from the $8.6B last post-money base. Peer-Median analysis across the 6-company cohort—including Figure AI at $39.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, Crusoe Energy at $10.0B, SandboxAQ at $5.8B, and Ayar Labs at $3.8B—supports a $7.9B median valuation. The wide dispersion in the peer set ($2.5B to $39.0B) reflects heterogeneity in business model maturity and capital deployment across the AI & Software frontier-tech landscape.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $6.7B to $14.8B, with a median of $10.4B. The current $8.6B valuation sits at the lower bound of this range. Key uncertainties include the timing and stage classification of future financing, the appropriate peer comparability given operational differences, and the valuation inflection common to downstream fundraising rounds in this sector.
